description: Simple bug fix — should trigger QUICK mode, not full plan
prompt: There's a typo in the button label on the login page. It says 'Singin' instead of 'Sign in'. Fix it.
assertions: Output uses quick mode (no full 7-section plan)
Output does NOT include a Skill Map table
Output does NOT include a Resume Prompt section
Output references the correct file or component to edit
Total output is concise — under 20 lines

description: Complex bug — should trigger full plan with systematic-debugging
prompt: Users are reporting that notifications aren't being delivered in real-time. Sometimes they appear after a 10-minute delay, sometimes never. This affects all users on the Pro plan. Help me plan how to debug and fix this.
assertions: Output uses FULL plan format with all 5 sections
Step 1 uses systematic-debugging skill
Plan mode is specified for investigation steps
Pre-flight check mentions clarifying questions (queue driver? broadcast config?)
Token safety note is present
Resume prompt is included

description: New feature — should trigger full plan with brainstorming → writing-plans flow
prompt: I need to add a real-time notification bell to the Laravel app. Users should see a badge count and be able to click to see recent notifications.
assertions: Output uses FULL plan format
Step sequence includes brainstorming, then writing-plans, then executing-plans
laravel-boost MCP failure is flagged with laravel-specialist fallback
Frontend steps reference ui-ux-pro-max and tailwindcss-development
TypeScript step ends with ts-check
Session end sequence is present: timeline-report → finishing-a-development-branch → commit-commands

description: Refactor task — should use git worktrees for isolation
prompt: I want to refactor the user authentication module to use Laravel Sanctum instead of the current session-based approach. Plan this out for me.
assertions: Output uses FULL plan format
using-git-worktrees skill is recommended for isolation
requesting-code-review appears after implementation
laravel-specialist and php-pro are included
A STOP checkpoint is present (this is a large refactor)
Resume prompt is included

description: UI-only task — should route to ui-ux-pro-max + frontend-design
prompt: The dashboard looks outdated. Redesign it to be more modern — better cards, cleaner typography, a sidebar nav.
assertions: ui-ux-pro-max is the lead skill
frontend-design and tailwindcss-development are included
Skill map table is present
skill-creator is mentioned as a check (should we build a project UI skill?)
ts-check appears at the end of the implementation phase

description: Spec file provided — should read and plan from it
assertions: Output acknowledges reading the spec file
Task summary reflects spec content (or asks user to paste if path isn't readable)
Full plan format is used
Pre-flight check lists any ambiguities from the spec

description: Trivial 1-step task — complexity gate must fire
prompt: Update the copyright year in the footer from 2024 to 2025.
assertions: Output does NOT use the full 7-section plan format
Response is short — a single prompt or 2-3 lines of guidance
No Skill Map table
No Resume Prompt section

description: First-time setup for a new project — should trigger Phase 0 init and Phase 1 tool discovery
prompt: I'm starting a new project. It's a React + Supabase app with TypeScript. Set up session coach for it and help me get going.
assertions: Phase 0 init command is shown (scripts/init_setup.py)
Phase 1 discovery is run and shows recommendations including typescript-lsp, supabase, and frontend-design
User is asked to confirm which tools to install before any install commands are issued
After the install phase, session planning begins (session.md is created or referenced)

description: User describes a task — skill should check setup freshness before planning
prompt: I want to plan adding a payment retry feature to my app. The system should retry failed payments up to 3 times with exponential backoff, notify the user on final failure.
assertions: Skill checks whether setup.md is current before proceeding to planning
Task is classified as Standard or Complex (not Quick) given multi-step nature
session.md is created with concrete steps for the payment retry feature
Step 1 prompt is copy-paste ready

description: User asks to install recommended tools — must show table and wait for confirmation before any installs
prompt: I have a TypeScript project with Next.js and Tailwind. What tools should I install for Claude Code? Install the ones that make sense.
assertions: A recommendations table is shown (plugin, reason, priority, status columns)
typescript-lsp and frontend-design appear in the table
User is explicitly asked which tools to approve before install commands are referenced
install_plugin.py commands are only shown after requesting user confirmation — not run silently
